steps.text: Two intersecting lines create four angles. Vertical angles are equal, and adjacent angles are supplementary (sum to \(180°\)). If one angle is \((9x - 140)°\), then its vertical angle is also \((9x - 140)°\), and each of the other two angles is \(180 - (9x - 140) = (320 - 9x)°\).

List all distinct sums of any two of the four angles: (1) two acute angles: \((9x-140)+(9x-140) = (18x-280)°\); (2) two obtuse angles: \((320-9x)+(320-9x) = (-18x+640)°\); (3) one of each (supplementary pair): \((9x-140)+(320-9x) = 180°\).
The only achievable sums are \((18x-280)°\), \((-18x+640)°\), and \(180°\). The expression \((-18x+280)°\) does not appear among them.

• A: \((-18x+280)°\) — not equal to any of the three achievable sums. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: \((-18x+640)°\) — equals the sum of the two obtuse angles. Achievable; eliminated.
  • C: \((18x-280)°\) — equals the sum of the two acute angles. Achievable; eliminated.
  • D: \(180°\) — equals the sum of one acute and one obtuse angle. Achievable; eliminated.
